data.description:
## Story S8: Orchestrator invocation contract in COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md + Value validation canary

### Description
**What**: Document the orchestrator's invocation contract for the new primitive in COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md: invoke `start` via `Bash(run_in_background: true)` → `bash_id`, drain progress via `BashOutput(bash_id)` for streaming only, poll `<state_dir>/done` for completion, read `<state_dir>/exit-code` as the authoritative result. Include an explicit "do not treat BashOutput return-empty as completion" note. Separately, execute the Value validation SC: on a canary worktree, run one representative test-gate cycle under both the legacy `test-batched` path and the new primitive on the same commit, record tool-call counts and total tokens consumed in the epic closure comment, assert the new path uses ≤3 tool calls and reduces tokens by ≥50%. INSTALL.md is also updated here with a pointer to the new test-run primitive.
**Why**: The orchestrator contract is the agent-facing API of the new primitive. Without clear documentation, every caller risks misusing `BashOutput` as a completion signal (one of the critical scenarios from brainstorm). The Value validation canary is the SC that gates epic closure on measured improvement.
**Scope**:
- IN: COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md contract section, INSTALL.md pointer, canary measurement script + fixture, closure comment with numbers
- OUT: CLAUDE.md full architecture update (S9 doc refresh)

### Done Definitions
- When this story is complete, COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md includes an "Orchestrator invocation contract" section describing `run_in_background` dispatch, `BashOutput` for progress-only, `done` marker polling, and `exit-code` read, with an explicit "do not treat BashOutput return-empty as completion" note
  ← Satisfies: "Orchestrator invocation contract — documented in COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md: the orchestrator invokes test-run start via Bash(run_in_background: true) ... Explicit 'do not treat BashOutput return-empty as completion' note"
- When this story is complete, a canary worktree runs one representative test-gate cycle under (a) the legacy path and (b) the new primitive on the same commit; both tool-call count and total tokens from dispatch to verdict are recorded in the epic closure comment
  ← Satisfies: "before closing the epic, a canary worktree runs one representative test-gate cycle under (a) the legacy test-batched path and (b) the new background primitive, on the same commit. The epic closure comment records both tool-call counts and total tokens consumed"
- When this story is complete, the closure comment documents that the new path completed in ≤3 tool calls AND reduced tokens by ≥50% vs the legacy path on the same suite
  ← Satisfies: "Acceptance: the new path completes in ≤3 tool calls (1 dispatch + ≤2 drains) and reduces tokens by ≥50% vs the legacy path on the same suite. Failure to meet either threshold blocks epic closure pending approach revision"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

### Considerations
- [Testing] The specific canary workload (suite or file-edit trigger) must be pinned and recorded alongside the numbers so future regression tests are comparable — reviewer-flagged minor refinement from brainstorm
- [Reliability] This is the SC that can block epic closure; run it before S9 docs update so docs reflect measured outcomes
## Escalation Policy
**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
data.title: As a DSO maintainer, orchestrator contract is documented and value validation canary proves token + tool-call improvements
